predicted_memories.summary: User values comprehensive context preservation in development work and wants detailed technical documentation including file names, code snippets, decisions, and errors encountered.
User is concerned about task continuity and wants explicit capture of user feedback, changing intent, and pending work to avoid losing critical context.

genai is a production-grade full-stack generative media platform with Laravel 12 API (Filament admin panel, OpenAPI spec), Vue 3 TypeScript portal (Pinia, vee-validate), and organized design docs folder.
genai/api has an 18KB OpenAPI specification available but lacks published auto-generated API documentation (Swagger UI/ReDoc); API developer experience could be improved by exposing this spec.
genai/portal is well-documented with comprehensive CLAUDE.md containing architecture guidelines, uses Vue 3, TypeScript, Pinia, vee-validate, OAuth backend flow, and BEM CSS methodology.
personal-site is an early-stage Astro 5.7 blog with 12 blog posts (YAML frontmatter), projects collection, deployed to Vercel using @astrojs/vercel adapter and Tailwind CSS v4.

xcanny/docs is a plain-markdown design documentation repository (no build tooling) containing decision logs and design specs for API, notifications, trending algorithm, account claiming, and email verification.
Giao is actively exploring documentation infrastructure and opportunities across multiple projects (personal-site, xcanny, genai) to assess maturity levels and identify improvement needs.

Completed Tasks 9-10 for Zettelgraph on 2026-04-21: ClustersPage visualizes graph clusters with multi-color palette and legend showing top 10 clusters; OrphansPage highlights isolated notes in red against dimmed gray background.
User works on genai/api Laravel project with Template models for managing provider configurations
User's code investigation approach is methodical and comprehensive: traces models → migrations → controllers → requests → forms → resources → seeders → tests → jobs to understand feature usage

User does structured code reviews using a systematic rubric: spec coverage, code correctness, test completeness, task ordering, file paths, and command validation. Critical issues (type safety, missing validation) are distinguished from medium issues.
User works on Laravel projects (v12) with Pest testing framework and Sail Docker setup. Uses structured feature tests and factory patterns.
When reviewing implementation plans, user prioritizes type safety validation and comprehensive edge-case testing. Marks missing edge-case tests as critical gaps, not minor issues.
Xcanny has marked (v17.0.1) installed in both app and admin. Admin additionally has Milkdown (v7.18.0) with GFM preset and Crepe WYSIWYG editor for content authoring.

Both apps use regex-based HTML sanitization (removing scripts/event handlers) rather than DOMPurify, despite DOMPurify being installed in admin. This is a security-relevant architectural decision worth standardizing.
Admin has existing /admin/src/utils/markdown.ts utility using marked with GFM enabled and basic regex sanitization. Can be reused or extended for post/comment markdown rendering.
Admin has MarkdownEditor.vue component (Milkdown Crepe WYSIWYG) with image upload to /uploads/images endpoint. Can serve as reference for implementing markdown editing UI.

Comment mention formats differ between app (@Name from displayBody) and admin (@[member:ID|Name]). App additionally uses linkifyText() for URL conversion. Asymmetry affects consistency of comment rendering across apps.
Completed Task 4: implemented CommentsResource for xcanny platform admin panel, following PostResource pattern with tests, table configuration, and proper navigation sort.
Pattern: When creating new Filament resources, follow exact structure of existing PostResource (Resource class, Tables class, List/View/Create/Edit pages).
Use vendor/bin/sail for all commands in xcanny Laravel project.
xcanny admin panel sidebar navigation order: Workspaces (sort 1), Posts (2), Comments (3), Members (4), PlatformAdmins (10).
Comment model uses HasSpamTracking trait with marked_spam_at and marked_spam_by fields; use includingSpam() scope in admin resources to bypass global scope so admins see all comments.
Filament resource file structure: Resource class in Resources/ResourceName.php, table configuration in Tables/TableName.php, pages in Pages/List/View/Create/Edit.php.
When creating new features, write comprehensive feature tests covering: page rendering, data display with relationships, and filtering behavior.

Task 12 (Migration Support) completed for Phileas. Created migrate.py with detect_existing_data() and create_default_config() functions, plus 7 comprehensive passing tests. Not yet committed as requested.

Phileas migration support detects pre-config installations by checking for memory.db, chroma/, graph/, and config.toml. detect_existing_data() returns a dict with has_data, has_sqlite, has_chroma, has_graph, has_config booleans; create_default_config() generates config.toml from template with home path interpolation.

Phileas default configuration values: recall (similarity_floor=0.5, relevance_floor=0.15, graph_boost=0.5, mmr_lambda=0.7, default_top_k=10) and scoring weights (relevance=0.55, importance=0.2, recency=0.15, access=0.1). Log level INFO, file max 5MB with 3 backups.
As of Apr 21, Phileas system has not captured any memories since Apr 14-15 (~5 day gap); recall only surfaced data from Apr 13-14, indicating potential ingest/memorize cycle failure.
Around Apr 14-15, Giao was in intensive work on Phileas involving site development and overnight memory-research push, before the subsequent system silence.
Apr 15 was a quiet, heads-down workday for Giao (described as 'thôi kệ vậy')—the last confirmed activity before system memory capture ceased.
Giao expects Phileas to proactively surface daily context and maintain automatic awareness of their activities without requiring explicit prompting.
Completed Task 7: JSONL ingestion parser with parse_session_jsonl() and find_unprocessed_sessions() functions; 3 tests passing, ready for Task 8.
JSONL parser handles mixed message content formats: strings directly, or lists of text blocks that are concatenated for unified content extraction.

Giao uses a systematic 0-100 scoring rubric to evaluate PR issues: 0=false positive, 25=unverified, 50=real but minor, 75=important/verified, 100=definite and frequent.

When evaluating code issues, Giao investigates thoroughly across multiple branches, commits, and files before rendering judgment; when unable to verify a claim exists in the codebase, appropriately downgrades confidence rather than assuming the issue is real.

The project uses Vue.js with Bootstrap 5 for modal components. BaseModal.vue implements focus trapping via Bootstrap 5's Modal class (_enforceFocus method), which automatically traps focus within the modal and restores it when closed.
User prioritizes accessibility features in UI components, specifically focus trapping and keyboard navigation in modals. Shows concern with investigation into modal implementation details.

HTML5 dialog element is the modern approach for modal implementation in 2026 web design, offering native accessibility features with full CSS customization (preferred over custom modals or browser confirm()).

User has built a personal Secretary skill (/secretary) with multiple modes: morning briefing, evening recap, weekly reflection, task dashboard, and time portfolio. Base directory: /home/ajuno/.claude/skills/secretary.
User's secretary integrates with Google Calendar, Google Workspace Tasks, and Taskwarrior for data gathering. Daily entries logged to ~/life/daily/, weekly reflections to ~/life/weekly/.
User has a checklist CLI system for routine checklists (e.g., 'leaving-for-office', 'before-bed') with contextual activation based on time of day.
User is actively job hunting with focus on remote roles, including resume rewriting and profile setup on Arc.dev, We Work Remotely, and YC.

Giao completed a comprehensive 20-question Claude Code quiz (2 questions per lesson across all 10 lessons) and scored 16/20, rated Proficient. Assessment taken as a mastery check after completing lessons.
Giao demonstrated strong understanding in 6 Claude Code areas: Slash Commands, Skills, Subagents, Checkpoints, Advanced Features, and CLI.
Giao has knowledge gaps in 3 Claude Code areas: Plugins (scored 0/2), Hooks (confusion between types and events), and MCP (unclear on specific functions like mcp serve).
Giao confused hook types (Command/HTTP/Prompt/Agent) with hook events (Pre/Post/Error/Filter) — worth clarifying that these are different dimensions of hooks.
Giao is unfamiliar with plugin architecture specifics: plugin manifest location (.claude-plugin/plugin.json, not plugin.yaml), and the CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT environment variable.
Giao was unclear that `claude mcp serve` makes Claude Code itself act as an MCP server for other applications (enables multi-agent orchestration), not just testing server connections.
Giao thought path-scoped rules belong in `src/api/CLAUDE.md`, but the correct mechanism is `.claude/rules/*.md` files with `paths:` frontmatter for path-specific scoping.
A lesson-quiz skill exists at `/home/ajuno/claude-howto/.claude/skills/lesson-quiz` covering 10 Claude Code lessons with interactive quizzes and per-question feedback.
The spotify_player CLI tool uses capital D as the keyboard shortcut to switch audio devices.
Two Claude Code plugins (`explanatory-output-style` and `learning-output-style`) cause SessionStart errors and have been disabled to resolve them.

Assistant: .
Margin is a markup added on top of provider cost before charging users. Three types exist: none (pass-through), percentage (multiply cost by 1 + margin_value), and fixed (add flat amount).
Pricing strategy: cheap standard models ($0.02–$0.04) use pass-through margin (no markup); expensive operations like high-res upscaling apply margin to buffer against provider price fluctuations.

User: do you know which file stores the script for this conversation in claude code?

Assistant: Yep — cuộc trò chuyện này đang được lưu ở:

`~/.claude/projects/-home-ajuno/7c2c08fd-d019-4212-a00e-233d90a62112.jsonl`

Claude Code lưu từng session dưới dạng một file `.jsonl` (JSON Lines — mỗi dòng là một message/event), đặt trong `~/.claude/projects/<cwd-encoded>/`. Phần `-home-ajuno` là cwd `/home/ajuno` được encode lại bằng cách thay `/` thành `-`.

File vừa được update lúc 16:22, khớp với thời điểm mình đang nói chuyện — đó chính là session hiện tại.
